{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_22cfa645fbd8410d9ded5037","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# ParseError Design for Failure Cases\n2\t\n3\t## Overview\n4\t\n5\tThis document describes the comprehensive ParseError type design and error handling strategy for YAML parsing failures in the ARMOR project. The design integrates with the Result<T, ParseError> pattern for type-safe error handling.\n6\t\n7\t## Design Goals\n8\t\n9\t1. **Clear Error Categorization**: Distinct error types for different failure modes\n10\t2. **Rich Context Information**: Line numbers, column positions, source snippets\n11\t3. **Type-Safe Error Handling**: Compile-time guarantees with Result<T, ParseError>\n12\t4. **Backward Compatibility**: Integration with existing error types\n13\t5. **Error Propagation Strategy**: Clear patterns for error transformation and chaining\n14\t\n15\t## Error Type Hierarchy\n16\t\n17\t```\n18\tEnhancedParseError (comprehensive error type)\n19\t├── Kind: ParseErrorKind (enum-style discriminator)\n20\t│   ├── Syntax (YAML syntax errors)\n21\t│   ├── Structure (structure errors like duplicate keys)\n22\t│   ├── TypeMismatch (type conversion errors)\n23\t│   ├── IO (file I/O errors)\n24\t│   ├── Validation (semantic validation errors)\n25\t│   ├── Schema (schema validation errors)\n26\t│   ├── Empty (empty file errors)\n27\t│   └── Unknown (unknown error types)\n28\t├── Context: ErrorContext (location and source info)\n29\t│   ├── Line (line number)\n30\t│   ├── Column (column number)\n31\t│   ├── Snippet (source line)\n32\t│   ├── SurroundingLines (context lines)\n33\t│   └── SnippetLineIndex (error line index)\n34\t├── Detail: ParseErrorDetail (error-specific details)\n35\t│   ├── Syntax fields (Expected, Found)\n36\t│   ├── Structure fields (DuplicateKey, Location)\n37\t│   ├── Type fields (FieldPath, ExpectedType, ActualType, Value)\n38\t│   ├── Validation fields (ConstraintType, Constraint)\n39\t│   └── Schema fields (SchemaPath, SchemaName)\n40\t└── Implements YAMLError interface (Code, YAMLErrorType, Context)\n41\t```\n42\t\n43\t## Files Created\n44\t\n45\t1. **parse_error_design.go** - EnhancedParseError implementation with:\n46\t   - ParseErrorKind enum for error categorization\n47\t   - ErrorContext for location and source snippets\n48\t   - ParseErrorDetail for error-specific information\n49\t   - Construction helpers for each error kind\n50\t   - Legacy conversion methods\n51\t   - Kind checker methods\n52\t\n53\t2. **parse_result.go** - Result<T, ParseError> pattern implementation with:\n54\t   - ParseResultWithError[T] generic type\n55\t   - Ok/Err construction functions\n56\t   - Unwrap methods with safety guarantees\n57\t   - Map/AndThen/OrElse chaining operations\n58\t   - Batch operation support\n59\t   - Type aliases for common use cases\n60\t\n61\t3. **parse_error_design_test.go** - Comprehensive error type tests\n62\t4. **parse_result_test.go** - Comprehensive Result pattern tests\n63\t\n64\t## Summary\n65\t\n66\tThe ParseError design provides comprehensive error handling for YAML parsing with clear categorization, rich context, and type-safe error handling patterns.\n67\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"0cb7ae92-a70d-45f5-aaa9-e782a67e14a4","uuid":"7cc953d7-4762-467a-a9e2-62aa36efbab7","timestamp":"2026-07-11T12:42:45.783Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/yamlutil/PARSE_ERROR_DESIGN.md","content":"# ParseError Design for Failure Cases\n\n## Overview\n\nThis document describes the comprehensive ParseError type design and error handling strategy for YAML parsing failures in the ARMOR project. The design integrates with the Result<T, ParseError> pattern for type-safe error handling.\n\n## Design Goals\n\n1. **Clear Error Categorization**: Distinct error types for different failure modes\n2. **Rich Context Information**: Line numbers, column positions, source snippets\n3. **Type-Safe Error Handling**: Compile-time guarantees with Result<T, ParseError>\n4. **Backward Compatibility**: Integration with existing error types\n5. **Error Propagation Strategy**: Clear patterns for error transformation and chaining\n\n## Error Type Hierarchy\n\n```\nEnhancedParseError (comprehensive error type)\n├── Kind: ParseErrorKind (enum-style discriminator)\n│   ├── Syntax (YAML syntax errors)\n│   ├── Structure (structure errors like duplicate keys)\n│   ├── TypeMismatch (type conversion errors)\n│   ├── IO (file I/O errors)\n│   ├── Validation (semantic validation errors)\n│   ├── Schema (schema validation errors)\n│   ├── Empty (empty file errors)\n│   └── Unknown (unknown error types)\n├── Context: ErrorContext (location and source info)\n│   ├── Line (line number)\n│   ├── Column (column number)\n│   ├── Snippet (source line)\n│   ├── SurroundingLines (context lines)\n│   └── SnippetLineIndex (error line index)\n├── Detail: ParseErrorDetail (error-specific details)\n│   ├── Syntax fields (Expected, Found)\n│   ├── Structure fields (DuplicateKey, Location)\n│   ├── Type fields (FieldPath, ExpectedType, ActualType, Value)\n│   ├── Validation fields (ConstraintType, Constraint)\n│   └── Schema fields (SchemaPath, SchemaName)\n└── Implements YAMLError interface (Code, YAMLErrorType, Context)\n```\n\n## Files Created\n\n1. **parse_error_design.go** - EnhancedParseError implementation with:\n   - ParseErrorKind enum for error categorization\n   - ErrorContext for location and source snippets\n   - ParseErrorDetail for error-specific information\n   - Construction helpers for each error kind\n   - Legacy conversion methods\n   - Kind checker methods\n\n2. **parse_result.go** - Result<T, ParseError> pattern implementation with:\n   - ParseResultWithError[T] generic type\n   - Ok/Err construction functions\n   - Unwrap methods with safety guarantees\n   - Map/AndThen/OrElse chaining operations\n   - Batch operation support\n   - Type aliases for common use cases\n\n3. **parse_error_design_test.go** - Comprehensive error type tests\n4. **parse_result_test.go** - Comprehensive Result pattern tests\n\n## Summary\n\nThe ParseError design provides comprehensive error handling for YAML parsing with clear categorization, rich context, and type-safe error handling patterns.\n","numLines":67,"startLine":1,"totalLines":67}}}

error types.\n14\ttype ParseErrorKind string\n15\t\n16\tconst (\n17\t\t// ParseErrorKindSyntax indicates YAML syntax errors (indentation, malformed YAML, etc.)\n18\t\tParseErrorKindSyntax ParseErrorKind = \"syntax\"\n19\t\n20\t\t// ParseErrorKindStructure indicates YAML structure errors (duplicate keys, invalid nesting, etc.)\n21\t\tParseErrorKindStructure ParseErrorKind = \"structure\"\n22\t\n23\t\t// ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch indicates type conversion errors (string to int, etc.)\n24\t\tParseErrorKindTypeMismatch ParseErrorKind = \"type_mismatch\"\n25\t\n26\t\t// ParseErrorKindIO indicates file I/O errors (not found, permission denied, etc.)\n27\t\tParseErrorKindIO ParseErrorKind = \"io\"\n28\t\n29\t\t// ParseErrorKindValidation indicates semantic validation errors (required fields, constraints, etc.)\n30\t\tParseErrorKindValidation ParseErrorKind = \"validation\"\n31\t\n32\t\t// ParseErrorKindSchema indicates schema validation ParseErrorKindUnknown indicates an unknown error type\n39\t\tParseErrorKindUnknown ParseErrorKind = \"unknown\"\n40\t)\n41\t\n42\t// ParseErrorDetail contains specific details for different error kinds.\n43\t// Only relevant fields are populated based on the ErrorKind.\n44\ttype ParseErrorDetail struct {\n45\t\t// Syntax-specific fields\n46\t\tExpected string // What was expected (for syntax errors)\n47\t\tFound    string // What was actually found\n48\t\n49\t\t// Structure-specific fields\n50\t\tDuplicateKey string // Name of duplicate key (for structure errors)\n51\t\tLocation     string // Nested path to error location\n52\t\n53\t\t// Type mismatch-specific fields\n54\t\tFieldPath    string // Dot-notation path to field (for type errors)\n55\t\tExpectedType string // Expected type\n56\t\tActualType   string // Actual type found\n57\t\tValue        string // Actual value that caused error\n58\t\n59\t\t// Validation-specific fields\n60\t\tConstraintType string // Type of file\n65\t\tSchemaName string // Name of schema\n66\t}\n67\t\n68\t// ErrorContext provides rich context about the error location.\n69\ttype ErrorContext struct {\n70\t\t// Line is the line number error occurred\n77\t\tSnippet string\n78\t\n79\t\t// SurroundingLines provides context around the error line\n80\t\tSurroundingLines []string\n81\t\n82\t\t// SnippetLineIndex is the index of the error line within SurroundingLines\n83\t\tSnippetLineIndex int\n84\t}\n85\t\n86\t// EnhancedParseError provides comprehensive error information for parsing failures.\n87\t//\n88\t// This enhanced error type includes:\n89\t// - Error kind categorization (syntax, structure, type mismatch, etc.)\n90\t// - Detailed error-specific information\n91\t// - Rich context including source snippets\n92\t// - Integration with Result<T, ParseError> pattern\n93\ttype EnhancedParseError struct {\n94\t\t// Kind categorizes the error type\n95\t\tKind ParseErrorKind\n96\t\n97\t\t// FilePath is the path to the file being parsed\n98\t\tFilePath string\n99\t\n100\t\t// Message is a human-readable error message\n101\t\tMessage string\n102\t\n103\t\t// Context provides location and source context information\n104\t\tLocationInfo ErrorContext\n105\t\n106\t\t// Detail contains error-specific details\n107\t\tDetail ParseErrorDetail\n108\t\n109\t\t// UnderlyingErr is the wrapped error for compatibility\n110\t\tUnderlyingErr error\n111\t}\n112\t\n113\t// Error implements the error interface.\n114\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) Error() string {\n115\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n116\t\n117\t\t// Build error header\n118\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Line > 0 {\n119\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s error in %s at line %d\", e.Kind, e.FilePath, e.LocationInfo.Line))\n120\t\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Column > 0 {\n121\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column %d\", e.LocationInfo.Column))\n122\t\t\t}\n123\t\t} else {\n124\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s error in %s\", e.Kind, e.FilePath))\n125\t\t}\n126\t\n127\t\t// Add message\n128\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", e.Message))\n129\t\n130\t\t// Add kind-specific details\n131\t\tswitch e.Kind {\n132\t\tcase ParseErrorKindSyntax:\n133\t\t\tif e.Detail.Expected != \"\" || e.Detail.Found != \"\" {\n134\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (expected: %s, found: %s)\", e.Detail.Expected, e.Detail.Found))\n135\t\t\t}\n136\t\tcase ParseErrorKindStructure:\n137\t\t\tif e.Detail.DuplicateKey != \"\" {\n138\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (duplicate key: %s\", e.Detail.DuplicateKey))\n139\t\t\t\tif e.Detail.Location != \"\" {\n140\t\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" at %s\", e.Detail.Location))\n141\t\t\t\t}\n142\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\")\")\n143\t\t\t}\n144\t\tcase ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch:\n145\t\t\tif e.Detail.FieldPath != \"\" {\n146\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (field: %s, expected: %s, got: %s\",\n147\t\t\t\t\te.Detail.FieldPath, e.Detail.ExpectedType, e.Detail.ActualType))\n148\t\t\t}\n149\t\tcase ParseErrorKindValidation:\n150\t\t\tif e.Detail.FieldPath != \"\" && e.Detail.Constraint != \"\" {\n151\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (field: %s, constraint: %s)\",\n152\t\t\t\t\te.Detail.FieldPath, e.Detail.Constraint))\n153\t\t\t}\n154\t\t}\n155\t\n156\t\treturn sb.String()\n157\t}\n158\t\n159\t// Unwrap returns the underlying error.\n160\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) Unwrap() error {\n161\t\treturn e.UnderlyingErr\n162\t}\n163\t\n164\t// Code returns the error code for programmatic handling.\n165\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) Code() ErrorCode {\n166\t\tswitch e.Kind {\n167\t\tcase ParseErrorKindSyntax:\n168\t\t\treturn ErrCodeInvalidSyntax\n169\t\tcase ParseErrorKindStructure:\n170\t\t\tif e.Detail.DuplicateKey != \"\" {\n171\t\t\t\treturn ErrCodeDuplicateKey\n172\t\t\t}\n173\t\t\treturn ErrCodeInvalidStructure\n174\t\tcase ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch:\n175\t\t\treturn ErrCodeTypeMismatch\n176\t\tcase ParseErrorKindIO:\n177\t\t\treturn ErrCodeFileIOError\n178\t\tcase ParseErrorKindValidation:\n179\t\t\treturn ErrCodeValidationFailed\n180\t\tcase ParseErrorKindSchema:\n181\t\t\treturn ErrCodeSchemaValidation\n182\t\tcase *EnhancedParseError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n191\t\tswitch e.Kind {\n192\t\tcase ParseErrorKindSyntax:\n193\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeSyntax\n194\t\tcase ParseErrorKindStructure:\n195\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeStructure\n196\t\tcase ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch:\n197\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeTypeMismatch\n198\t\tcase ParseErrorKindIO:\n199\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeIO\n200\t\tcase ErrorTypeEmpty\n206\t\tdefault:\n207\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeUnknown\n208\t\t}\n209\t}\n210\t\n211\t// Context implements YAMLError interface.\n212\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) Context() string {\n213\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n214\t\n215\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Line > 0 {\n216\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"line: %d\", e.LocationInfo.Line))\n217\t\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Column > 0 {\n218\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column: %d\", e.LocationInfo.Column))\n219\t\t\t}\n220\t\t}\n221\t\n222\t\tif e.Detail.FieldPath != \"\" {\n223\t\t\tif sb.Len() > 0 {\n224\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\", \")\n225\t\t\t}\n226\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"field: %s\", e.Detail.FieldPath))\n227\t\t}\n228\t\n229\t\tif e.Detail.Location != \"\" {\n230\t\t\tif sb.Len() > 0 {\n231\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\", \")\n232\t\t\t}\n233\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"location: %s\", e.Detail.Location))\n234\t\t}\n235\t\n236\t\treturn sb.String()\n237\t}\n238\t\n239\t// String returns a detailed multi-line error representation with source snippet.\n240\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) String() string {\n241\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n242\t\n243\t\t// Error header\n244\t\tsb.WriteString(e.Error())\n245\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n246\t\n247\t\t// Add snippet if available\n248\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Snippet != \"\" {\n249\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n250\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"  \")\n251\t\t\tsb.WriteString(e.LocationInfo.Snippet)\n252\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n253\t\n254\t\t\t// Add column indicator if available\n255\t\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Column > 0 && e.LocationInfo.Column <= len(e.LocationInfo.Snippet) {\n256\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"  \")\n257\t\t\t\tfor i := 0; i < e.LocationInfo.Column-1; i++ {\n258\t\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\" \")\n259\t\t\t\t}\n260\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"^--- here\\n\")\n261\t\t\t}\n262\t\t}\n263\t\n264\t\t// Add surrounding context if available\n265\t\tif len(e.LocationInfo.SurroundingLines) > 0 {\n266\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n267\t\t\tfor i, line := range e.LocationInfo.SurroundingLines {\n268\t\t\t\tif i == e.LocationInfo.SnippetLineIndex {\n269\t\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"> \")\n270\t\t\t\t} else {\n271\t\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"  \")\n272\t\t\t\t}\n273\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(line)\n274\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n275\t\t\t}\n276\t\t}\n277\t\n278\t\treturn sb.String()\n279\t}\n280\t\n281\t// IsSyntaxError checks if this is a syntax error.\n282\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsSyntaxError() bool {\n283\t\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindSyntax\n284\t}\n285\t\n286\t// IsStructureError checks if this is a structure error.\n287\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsStructureError() bool {\n288\t\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindStructure\n289\t}\n290\t\n291\t// IsTypeMismatchError checks if this is a type mismatch error.\n292\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsTypeMismatchError() bool {\n293\t\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch\n294\t}\n295\t\n296\t// IsIOError checks if this is an I/O error.\n297\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsIOError() bool {\n298\t\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindIO\n299\t}\n300\t\n301\t// IsValidationError checks if this is a validation error.\n302\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsValidationError() bool {\n303\t\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindValidation\n304\t}\n305\t\n306\t// IsSchemaError checks if this is a schema error.\n307\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsSchemaError() bool {\n308\t\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindSchema\n309\t}\n310\t\n311\t// IsEmpty checks if this is an empty file error.\n312\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsEmpty() bool {\n313\t\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindEmpty\n314\t}\n315\t\n316\t// ============================================================================\n317\t// Error Construction Helpers\n318\t// ============================================================================\n319\t\n320\t// NewSyntaxParseError creates a new syntax error.\n321\tfunc NewSyntaxParseError(filePath, message string, line, column int, expected, found string) *EnhancedParseError {\n322\t\treturn &EnhancedParseError{\n323\t\t\tKind:     ParseErrorKindSyntax,\n324\t\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n325\t\t\tMessage:  message,\n326\t\t\tLocationInfo: ErrorContext{\n327\t\t\t\tLine:   line,\n328\t\t\t\tColumn: column,\n329\t\t\t},\n330\t\t\tDetail: ParseErrorDetail{\n331\t\t\t\tExpected: expected,\n332\t\t\t\tFound:    found,\n333\t\t\t},\n334\t\t}\n335\t}\n336\t\n337\t// NewStructureParseError creates a new structure error.\n338\tfunc NewStructureParseError(filePath, message string, line int, duplicateKey, location string) *EnhancedParseError {\n339\t\treturn &EnhancedParseError{\n340\t\t\tKind:     ParseErrorKindStructure,\n341\t\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n342\t\t\tMessage:  message,\n343\t\t\tLocationInfo: ErrorContext{\n344\t\t\t\tLine: line,\n345\t\t\t},\n346\t\t\tDetail: ParseErrorDetail{\n347\t\t\t\tDuplicateKey: duplicateKey,\n348\t\t\t\tLocation:     location,\n349\t\t\t},\n350\t\t}\n351\t}\n352\t\n353\t// NewTypeMismatchParseError creates a new type mismatch error.\n354\tfunc NewTypeMismatchParseError(filePath, message string, line int, fieldPath, expectedType, actualType, value string) *EnhancedParseError {\n355\t\treturn &EnhancedParseError{\n356\t\t\tKind:     ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch,\n357\t\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n358\t\t\tMessage:  message,\n359\t\t\tLocationInfo: ErrorContext{\n360\t\t\t\tLine: line,\n361\t\t\t},\n362\t\t\tDetail: ParseErrorDetail{\n363\t\t\t\tFieldPath:    fieldPath,\n364\t\t\t\tExpectedType: expectedType,\n365\t\t\t\tActualType:   actualType,\n366\t\t\t\tValue:        value,\n367\t\t\t},\n368\t\t}\n369\t}\n370\t\n371\t// NewIOParseError creates a new constraintType,\n396\t\t\t\tConstraint:     constraint,\n397\t\t\t},\n398\t\t}\n399\t}\n400\t\n401\t// NewSchemaParseError creates a new schema error.\n402\tfunc NewSchemaParseError(filePath, message schemaName,\n413\t\t\t},\n414\t\t}\n415\t}\n416\t\n417\t// NewEmptyParseError creates a new empty file error.\n418\tfunc NewEmptyParseError(filePath string) *EnhancedParseError {\n419\t\treturn &EnhancedParseError{\n420\t\t\tKind:     ParseErrorKindEmpty,\n421\t\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n422\t\t\tMessage:  \"file is empty or contains only whitespace\",\n423\t\t}\n424\t}\n425\t\n426\t// ============================================================================\n427\t// Error Propagation Strategy\n428\t// ============================================================================\n429\t\n430\t// The error propagation strategy for ParseError follows these principles:\n431\t//\n432\t// 1. Always provide rich context: Include line numbers, column positions, and\n433\t//    source snippets whenever possible.\n434\t//\n435\t// 2. Preserve error chain: Use error wrapping (errors.Wrap, fmt.Errorf with %w)\n436\t//    to maintain the full error chain for debugging.\n437\t//\n438\t// 3. Type-safe error handling: Use Result[T, ParseError] pattern for compile-time\n439\t//    error handling guarantees.\n440\t//\n441\t// 4. Error transformation: Transform lower-level errors (e.g., yaml.v3 errors)\n442\t//    into EnhancedParseError with appropriate context and snippets.\n443\t//\n444\t// 5. Fail-fast on critical errors: I/O errors and syntax errors should immediately\n445\t//    return errors without attempting further processing.\n446\t//\n447\t// Error Flow:\n448\t//   Raw Error → EnhancedParseError (with context) → Result[T, ParseError]\n449\t//\n450\t// Example propagation:\n451\t//   yaml.Unmarshal error → extract line/column → read source snippet →\n452\t//   NewSyntaxParseError with snippet → Result[T, ParseError]\n453\t\n454\t// ============================================================================\n455\t// Integration with Legacy Error Types\n456\t// ============================================================================\n457\t\n458\t// ToLegacySyntaxError converts EnhancedParseError to legacy SyntaxError.\n459\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) ToLegacySyntaxError() *SyntaxError {\n460\t\tif e.Kind != ParseErrorKindSyntax {\n461\t\t\treturn nil\n462\t\t}\n463\t\treturn &SyntaxError{\n464\t\t\tFilePath:  e.FilePath,\n465\t\t\tLine:      e.LocationInfo.Line,\n466\t\t\tColumn:    e.LocationInfo.Column,\n467\t\t\tMessage:   e.Message,\n468\t\t\tExpected:  e.Detail.Expected,\n469\t\t\tFound:     e.Detail.Found,\n470\t\t\tErr:       e.UnderlyingErr,\n471\t\t\tErrorCode: e.Code(),\n472\t\t}\n473\t}\n474\t\n475\t// ToLegacyStructureError converts EnhancedParseError to legacy StructureError.\n476\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) ToLegacyStructureError() *StructureError {\n477\t\tif e.Kind != ParseErrorKindStructure {\n478\t\t\treturn nil\n479\t\t}\n480\t\treturn &StructureError{\n481\t\t\tFilePath:     e.FilePath,\n482\t\t\tLine:         e.LocationInfo.Line,\n483\t\t\tMessage:      e.Message,\n484\t\t\tDuplicateKey: e.Detail.DuplicateKey,\n485\t\t\tLocation:     e.Detail.Location,\n486\t\t\tErr:          e.UnderlyingErr,\n487\t\t\tErrorCode:    e.Code(),\n488\t\t}\n489\t}\n490\t\n491\t// ToLegacyTypeMismatchError converts EnhancedParseError to legacy TypeMismatchError.\n492\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) ToLegacyTypeMismatchError() *TypeMismatchError {\n493\t\tif e.Kind != ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch {\n494\t\t\treturn nil\n495\t\t}\n496\t\treturn &TypeMismatchError{\n497\t\t\tFilePath:     e.FilePath,\n498\t\t\tFieldPath:    e.Detail.FieldPath,\n499\t\t\tExpectedType: e.Detail.ExpectedType,\n500\t\t\tActualType:   e.Detail.ActualType,\n501\t\t\tValue:        e.Detail.Value,\n502\t\t\tLine:         e.LocationInfo.Line,\n503\t\t\tErrorCode:    e.Code(),\n504\t\t}\n505\t}\n506\t\n507\t// ToLegacyParseError converts EnhancedParseError to legacy ParseError.\n508\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) ToLegacyParseError() *ParseError {\n509\t\treturn &ParseError{\n510\t\t\tFilePath:   e.FilePath,\n511\t\t\tLine:       e.LocationInfo.Line,\n512\t\t\tColumn:     e.LocationInfo.Column,\n513\t\t\tMessage:    e.Message,\n514\t\t\tContextStr: e.Context(),\n515\t\t\tErr:        e.UnderlyingErr,\n516\t\t\tErrorType:  e.YAMLErrorType(),\n517\t\t\tErrorCode:  e.Code(),\n518\t\t}\n519\t}\n520\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"0cb7ae92-a70d-45f5-aaa9-e782a67e14a4","uuid":"c3b5ce9a-87fe-4935-946d-c73579983ca8","timestamp":"2026-07-11T12:42:45.887Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/yamlutil/parse_error_design.go","content":"// Package yamlutil provides enhanced ParseError type design for failure cases.\n//\n// This file implements the comprehensive ParseError design that integrates\n// with the Result<T, ParseError> pattern for type-safe error handling.\npackage yamlutil\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n// ParseErrorKind represents the category of parse error.\n// This serves as an enum-style discriminator for different error types.\ntype ParseErrorKind string\n\nconst (\n\t// ParseErrorKindSyntax indicates YAML syntax errors (indentation, malformed YAML, etc.)\n\tParseErrorKindSyntax ParseErrorKind = \"syntax\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorKindStructure indicates YAML structure errors (duplicate keys, invalid nesting, etc.)\n\tParseErrorKindStructure ParseErrorKind = \"structure\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch indicates type conversion errors (string to int, etc.)\n\tParseErrorKindTypeMismatch ParseErrorKind = \"type_mismatch\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorKindIO indicates file I/O errors (not found, permission denied, etc.)\n\tParseErrorKindIO ParseErrorKind = \"io\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorKindValidation indicates semantic validation errors (required fields, constraints, etc.)\n\tParseErrorKindValidation ParseErrorKind = \"validation\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorKindSchema indicates schema validation errors\n\tParseErrorKindSchema ParseErrorKind = \"schema\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorKindEmpty indicates empty file or content\n\tParseErrorKindEmpty ParseErrorKind = \"empty\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorKindUnknown indicates an unknown error type\n\tParseErrorKindUnknown ParseErrorKind = \"unknown\"\n)\n\n// ParseErrorDetail contains specific details for different error kinds.\n// Only relevant fields are populated based on the ErrorKind.\ntype ParseErrorDetail struct {\n\t// Syntax-specific fields\n\tExpected string // What was expected (for syntax errors)\n\tFound    string // What was actually found\n\n\t// Structure-specific fields\n\tDuplicateKey string // Name of duplicate key (for structure errors)\n\tLocation     string // Nested path to error location\n\n\t// Type mismatch-specific fields\n\tFieldPath    string // Dot-notation path to field (for type errors)\n\tExpectedType string // Expected type\n\tActualType   string // Actual type found\n\tValue        string // Actual value that caused error\n\n\t// Validation-specific fields\n\tConstraintType string // Type of constraint (range, length, pattern, etc.)\n\tConstraint     string // Description of constraint\n\n\t// Schema-specific fields\n\tSchemaPath string // Path to schema file\n\tSchemaName string // Name of schema\n}\n\n// ErrorContext provides rich context about the error location.\ntype ErrorContext struct {\n\t// Line is the line number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n\tLine int\n\n\t// Column is the column number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n\tColumn int\n\n\t// Snippet is the line of source code where error occurred\n\tSnippet string\n\n\t// SurroundingLines provides context around the error line\n\tSurroundingLines []string\n\n\t// SnippetLineIndex is the index of the error line within SurroundingLines\n\tSnippetLineIndex int\n}\n\n// EnhancedParseError provides comprehensive error information for parsing failures.\n//\n// This enhanced error type includes:\n// - Error kind categorization (syntax, structure, type mismatch, etc.)\n// - Detailed error-specific information\n// - Rich context including source snippets\n// - Integration with Result<T, ParseError> pattern\ntype EnhancedParseError struct {\n\t// Kind categorizes the error type\n\tKind ParseErrorKind\n\n\t// FilePath is the path to the file being parsed\n\tFilePath string\n\n\t// Message is a human-readable error message\n\tMessage string\n\n\t// Context provides location and source context information\n\tLocationInfo ErrorContext\n\n\t// Detail contains error-specific details\n\tDetail ParseErrorDetail\n\n\t// UnderlyingErr is the wrapped error for compatibility\n\tUnderlyingErr error\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) Error() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\n\t// Build error header\n\tif e.LocationInfo.Line > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s error in %s at line %d\", e.Kind, e.FilePath, e.LocationInfo.Line))\n\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Column > 0 {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column %d\", e.LocationInfo.Column))\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s error in %s\", e.Kind, e.FilePath))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add message\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", e.Message))\n\n\t// Add kind-specific details\n\tswitch e.Kind {\n\tcase ParseErrorKindSyntax:\n\t\tif e.Detail.Expected != \"\" || e.Detail.Found != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (expected: %s, found: %s)\", e.Detail.Expected, e.Detail.Found))\n\t\t}\n\tcase ParseErrorKindStructure:\n\t\tif e.Detail.DuplicateKey != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (duplicate key: %s\", e.Detail.DuplicateKey))\n\t\t\tif e.Detail.Location != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" at %s\", e.Detail.Location))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\")\")\n\t\t}\n\tcase ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch:\n\t\tif e.Detail.FieldPath != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (field: %s, expected: %s, got: %s\",\n\t\t\t\te.Detail.FieldPath, e.Detail.ExpectedType, e.Detail.ActualType))\n\t\t}\n\tcase ParseErrorKindValidation:\n\t\tif e.Detail.FieldPath != \"\" && e.Detail.Constraint != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (field: %s, constraint: %s)\",\n\t\t\t\te.Detail.FieldPath, e.Detail.Constraint))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the underlying error.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) Unwrap() error {\n\treturn e.UnderlyingErr\n}\n\n// Code returns the error code for programmatic handling.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tswitch e.Kind {\n\tcase ParseErrorKindSyntax:\n\t\treturn ErrCodeInvalidSyntax\n\tcase ParseErrorKindStructure:\n\t\tif e.Detail.DuplicateKey != \"\" {\n\t\t\treturn ErrCodeDuplicateKey\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn ErrCodeInvalidStructure\n\tcase ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch:\n\t\treturn ErrCodeTypeMismatch\n\tcase ParseErrorKindIO:\n\t\treturn ErrCodeFileIOError\n\tcase ParseErrorKindValidation:\n\t\treturn ErrCodeValidationFailed\n\tcase ParseErrorKindSchema:\n\t\treturn ErrCodeSchemaValidation\n\tcase ParseErrorKindEmpty:\n\t\treturn ErrCodeFileEmpty\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn ErrCodeParseError\n\t}\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\tswitch e.Kind {\n\tcase ParseErrorKindSyntax:\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeSyntax\n\tcase ParseErrorKindStructure:\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeStructure\n\tcase ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch:\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeTypeMismatch\n\tcase ParseErrorKindIO:\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeIO\n\tcase ParseErrorKindValidation:\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeValidation\n\tcase ParseErrorKindSchema:\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeSchema\n\tcase ParseErrorKindEmpty:\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeEmpty\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeUnknown\n\t}\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) Context() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\n\tif e.LocationInfo.Line > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"line: %d\", e.LocationInfo.Line))\n\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Column > 0 {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column: %d\", e.LocationInfo.Column))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif e.Detail.FieldPath != \"\" {\n\t\tif sb.Len() > 0 {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\", \")\n\t\t}\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"field: %s\", e.Detail.FieldPath))\n\t}\n\n\tif e.Detail.Location != \"\" {\n\t\tif sb.Len() > 0 {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\", \")\n\t\t}\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"location: %s\", e.Detail.Location))\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// String returns a detailed multi-line error representation with source snippet.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) String() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\n\t// Error header\n\tsb.WriteString(e.Error())\n\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n\n\t// Add snippet if available\n\tif e.LocationInfo.Snippet != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\"  \")\n\t\tsb.WriteString(e.LocationInfo.Snippet)\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n\n\t\t// Add column indicator if available\n\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Column > 0 && e.LocationInfo.Column <= len(e.LocationInfo.Snippet) {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"  \")\n\t\t\tfor i := 0; i < e.LocationInfo.Column-1; i++ {\n\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\" \")\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"^--- here\\n\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Add surrounding context if available\n\tif len(e.LocationInfo.SurroundingLines) > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n\t\tfor i, line := range e.LocationInfo.SurroundingLines {\n\t\t\tif i == e.LocationInfo.SnippetLineIndex {\n\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"> \")\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"  \")\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(line)\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// IsSyntaxError checks if this is a syntax error.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsSyntaxError() bool {\n\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindSyntax\n}\n\n// IsStructureError checks if this is a structure error.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsStructureError() bool {\n\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindStructure\n}\n\n// IsTypeMismatchError checks if this is a type mismatch error.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsTypeMismatchError() bool {\n\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch\n}\n\n// IsIOError checks if this is an I/O error.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsIOError() bool {\n\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindIO\n}\n\n// IsValidationError checks if this is a validation error.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsValidationError() bool {\n\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindValidation\n}\n\n// IsSchemaError checks if this is a schema error.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsSchemaError() bool {\n\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindSchema\n}\n\n// IsEmpty checks if this is an empty file error.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsEmpty() bool {\n\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindEmpty\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Error Construction Helpers\n// ============================================================================\n\n// NewSyntaxParseError creates a new syntax error.\nfunc NewSyntaxParseError(filePath, message string, line, column int, expected, found string) *EnhancedParseError {\n\treturn &EnhancedParseError{\n\t\tKind:     ParseErrorKindSyntax,\n\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n\t\tMessage:  message,\n\t\tLocationInfo: ErrorContext{\n\t\t\tLine:   line,\n\t\t\tColumn: column,\n\t\t},\n\t\tDetail: ParseErrorDetail{\n\t\t\tExpected: expected,\n\t\t\tFound:    found,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewStructureParseError creates a new structure error.\nfunc NewStructureParseError(filePath, message string, line int, duplicateKey, location string) *EnhancedParseError {\n\treturn &EnhancedParseError{\n\t\tKind:     ParseErrorKindStructure,\n\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n\t\tMessage:  message,\n\t\tLocationInfo: ErrorContext{\n\t\t\tLine: line,\n\t\t},\n\t\tDetail: ParseErrorDetail{\n\t\t\tDuplicateKey: duplicateKey,\n\t\t\tLocation:     location,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewTypeMismatchParseError creates a new type mismatch error.\nfunc NewTypeMismatchParseError(filePath, message string, line int, fieldPath, expectedType, actualType, value string) *EnhancedParseError {\n\treturn &EnhancedParseError{\n\t\tKind:     ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch,\n\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n\t\tMessage:  message,\n\t\tLocationInfo: ErrorContext{\n\t\t\tLine: line,\n\t\t},\n\t\tDetail: ParseErrorDetail{\n\t\t\tFieldPath:    fieldPath,\n\t\t\tExpectedType: expectedType,\n\t\t\tActualType:   actualType,\n\t\t\tValue:        value,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewIOParseError creates a new I/O error.\nfunc NewIOParseError(filePath, message string, line int, underlyingErr error) *EnhancedParseError {\n\treturn &EnhancedParseError{\n\t\tKind:          ParseErrorKindIO,\n\t\tFilePath:      filePath,\n\t\tMessage:       message,\n\t\tUnderlyingErr: underlyingErr,\n\t\tLocationInfo: ErrorContext{\n\t\t\tLine: line,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewValidationParseError creates a new validation error.\nfunc NewValidationParseError(filePath, message string, line int, fieldPath, constraintType, constraint string) *EnhancedParseError {\n\treturn &EnhancedParseError{\n\t\tKind:     ParseErrorKindValidation,\n\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n\t\tMessage:  message,\n\t\tLocationInfo: ErrorContext{\n\t\t\tLine: line,\n\t\t},\n\t\tDetail: ParseErrorDetail{\n\t\t\tFieldPath:      fieldPath,\n\t\t\tConstraintType: constraintType,\n\t\t\tConstraint:     constraint,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewSchemaParseError creates a new schema error.\nfunc NewSchemaParseError(filePath, message string, line int, schemaPath, schemaName string) *EnhancedParseError {\n\treturn &EnhancedParseError{\n\t\tKind:     ParseErrorKindSchema,\n\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n\t\tMessage:  message,\n\t\tLocationInfo: ErrorContext{\n\t\t\tLine: line,\n\t\t},\n\t\tDetail: ParseErrorDetail{\n\t\t\tSchemaPath: schemaPath,\n\t\t\tSchemaName: schemaName,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewEmptyParseError creates a new empty file error.\nfunc NewEmptyParseError(filePath string) *EnhancedParseError {\n\treturn &EnhancedParseError{\n\t\tKind:     ParseErrorKindEmpty,\n\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n\t\tMessage:  \"file is empty or contains only whitespace\",\n\t}\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Error Propagation Strategy\n// ============================================================================\n\n// The error propagation strategy for ParseError follows these principles:\n//\n// 1. Always provide rich context: Include line numbers, column positions, and\n//    source snippets whenever possible.\n//\n// 2. Preserve error chain: Use error wrapping (errors.Wrap, fmt.Errorf with %w)\n//    to maintain the full error chain for debugging.\n//\n// 3. Type-safe error handling: Use Result[T, ParseError] pattern for compile-time\n//    error handling guarantees.\n//\n// 4. Error transformation: Transform lower-level errors (e.g., yaml.v3 errors)\n//    into EnhancedParseError with appropriate context and snippets.\n//\n// 5. Fail-fast on critical errors: I/O errors and syntax errors should immediately\n//    return errors without attempting further processing.\n//\n// Error Flow:\n//   Raw Error → EnhancedParseError (with context) → Result[T, ParseError]\n//\n// Example propagation:\n//   yaml.Unmarshal error → extract line/column → read source snippet →\n//   NewSyntaxParseError with snippet → Result[T, ParseError]\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Integration with Legacy Error Types\n// ============================================================================\n\n// ToLegacySyntaxError converts EnhancedParseError to legacy SyntaxError.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) ToLegacySyntaxError() *SyntaxError {\n\tif e.Kind != ParseErrorKindSyntax {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\treturn &SyntaxError{\n\t\tFilePath:  e.FilePath,\n\t\tLine:      e.LocationInfo.Line,\n\t\tColumn:    e.LocationInfo.Column,\n\t\tMessage:   e.Message,\n\t\tExpected:  e.Detail.Expected,\n\t\tFound:     e.Detail.Found,\n\t\tErr:       e.UnderlyingErr,\n\t\tErrorCode: e.Code(),\n\t}\n}\n\n// ToLegacyStructureError converts EnhancedParseError to legacy StructureError.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) ToLegacyStructureError() *StructureError {\n\tif e.Kind != ParseErrorKindStructure {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\treturn &StructureError{\n\t\tFilePath:     e.FilePath,\n\t\tLine:         e.LocationInfo.Line,\n\t\tMessage:      e.Message,\n\t\tDuplicateKey: e.Detail.DuplicateKey,\n\t\tLocation:     e.Detail.Location,\n\t\tErr:          e.UnderlyingErr,\n\t\tErrorCode:    e.Code(),\n\t}\n}\n\n// ToLegacyTypeMismatchError converts EnhancedParseError to legacy TypeMismatchError.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) ToLegacyTypeMismatchError() *TypeMismatchError {\n\tif e.Kind != ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\treturn &TypeMismatchError{\n\t\tFilePath:     e.FilePath,\n\t\tFieldPath:    e.Detail.FieldPath,\n\t\tExpectedType: e.Detail.ExpectedType,\n\t\tActualType:   e.Detail.ActualType,\n\t\tValue:        e.Detail.Value,\n\t\tLine:         e.LocationInfo.Line,\n\t\tErrorCode:    e.Code(),\n\t}\n}\n\n// ToLegacyParseError converts EnhancedParseError to legacy ParseError.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) tt.wantType)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif got := tt.err.Context(); got != tt.wantContext {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Context() = %v, want %v\", got, tt.wantContext)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// EnhancedParseError Kind Checking Tests\n// ============================================================================\n\nfunc TestEnhancedParseErrorKindCheckers(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname      string\n\t\terr       *EnhancedParseError\n\t\tcheckFunc func(*EnhancedParseError) bool\n\t\twant      bool\n\t}{\n\t\t{\"syntax error is syntax\", NewSyntaxParseError(\"test.yaml\", \"err\", 1, 1, \"\", \"\"),\n\t\t\t(*EnhancedParseError).IsSyntaxError, true},\n\t\t{\"syntax error is not structure\", NewSyntaxParseError(\"test.yaml\", \"err\", 1, 1, \"\", \"\"),\n\t\t\t(*EnhancedParseError).IsStructureError, false},\n\t\t{\"structure error is structure\", NewStructureParseError(\"test.yaml\", \"err\", 1, \"\", \"\"),\n\t\t\t(*EnhancedParseError).IsStructureError, true},\n\t\t{\"structure error is not syntax\", NewStructureParseError(\"test.yaml\", \"err\", 1, \"\", \"\"),\n\t\t\t(*EnhancedParseError).IsSyntaxError, false},\n\t\t{\"type mismatch is type mismatch\", NewTypeMismatchParseError(\"test.yaml\", \"err\", 1, \"\", \"\", \"\", \"\"),\n\t\t\t(*EnhancedParseError).IsTypeMismatchError, true},\n\t\t{\"IO error is IO\", NewIOParseError(\"test.yaml\", \"err\", 0, nil),\n\t\t\t(*EnhancedParseError).IsIOError, true},\n\t\t{\"validation error is validation\", NewValidationParseError(\"test.yaml\", \"err\", 1, \"\", \"\", \"\"),\n\t\t\t(*EnhancedParseError).IsValidationError, true},\n\t\t{\"schema error is schema\", NewSchemaParseError(\"test.yaml\", \"err\", 1, \"\", \"\"),\n\t\t\t(*EnhancedParseError).IsSchemaError, true},\n\t\t{\"empty error is empty\", NewEmptyParseError(\"test.yaml\"),\n\t\t\t(*EnhancedParseError).IsEmpty, true},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tif got := tt.checkFunc(tt.err); got != tt.want {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"check function returned %v, want %v\", got, tt.want)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// EnhancedParseError Legacy Conversion Tests\n// ============================================================================\n\nfunc TestEnhancedParseErrorToLegacyConversions(t *testing.T) {\n\tt.Run(\"to syntax error\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tenhanced := NewSyntaxParseError(\"test.yaml\", \"bad syntax\", 5, 10, \":\", \"}\")\n\t\tlegacy := enhanced.ToLegacySyntaxError()\n\n\t\tif legacy == nil {\n\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"expected non-nil SyntaxError\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif legacy.FilePath != \"test.yaml\" {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected FilePath='test.yaml', got '%s'\", legacy.FilePath)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif legacy.Line != 5 {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected Line=5, got %d\", legacy.Line)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif legacy.Column != 10 {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected Column=10, got %d\", \"\", \"\")\n\t\tlegacy := enhanced.ToLegacySyntaxError()\n\n\t\tif legacy != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Error(\"expected nil for non-syntax error conversion\")\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"to structure error\", ParseResultWithError[T]) IsOk() bool {\n\treturn r.success\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the successful data.\n// Panics if the result is an error - always check IsOk() first.\nfunc (r handling.\n2\t//\n3\t// The Result[T, E] type represents either success (Ok) containing a value of type T,\n4\t// or failure (Err) containing an error of type E.\n5\t//\n6\t// This pattern enables explicit error handling without exceptions, making error\n7\t// paths visible in the type system and forcing proper error handling.\n8\tpackage yamlutil\n9\t\n10\timport \"fmt\"\n11\t\n12\t// Result represents the outcome of an operation that can either succeed with a value\n13\t// or fail with an error.\n14\t//\n15\t// Result is modeled after Rust's Result<T, E> type and provides a type-safe way to\n16\t// handle operations that can fail without using exceptions.\n17\t//\n18\t// Type parameters:\n19\t//   - T: The success value type\n20\t//   - E: The error type (typically ParseError, ValidationError, or any YAMLError)\n21\t//\n22\t// Example usage:\n23\t//\n24\t//\t// A function that returns Result[Config, ParseError]\n25\t//\tfunc ParseConfig(path string) Result[Config, ParseError] {\n26\t//\t    data, err := os.ReadFile(path)\n27\t//\t    if err != nil {\n28\t//\t        return Err[Config](ParseError{...})\n29\t//\t    }\n30\t//\t    config := parseConfig(data)\n31\t//\t    return Ok[Config](config)\n32\t//\t}\n33\t//\n34\t//\t// Using the result\n35\t//\tresult := ParseConfig(\"config.yaml\")\n36\t//\tif result.IsOk() {\n37\t//\t    config := result.Unwrap()\n38\t//\t    // Use config\n39\t//\t} else {\n40\t//\t    err := result.UnwrapErr()\n41\t//\t    // Handle error\n42\t//\t}\n43\ttype Result[T any, E any] struct {\n44\t\t// ok is true when the result is successful\n45\t\tok bool\n46\t\n47\t\t// value contains the success value when ok is true\n48\t\tvalue T\n49\t\n50\t\t// err contains the error when ok is false\n51\t\terr E\n52\t}\n53\t\n54\t// Ok creates a successful Result containing the provided value.\n55\t//\n56\t// Example:\n57\t//\tresult := Ok(42)\n58\t//\tresult := Ok(\"success\")\n59\t//\tresult := Ok(myStruct{})\n60\tfunc Ok[T any, E any](value T) Result[T, E] {\n61\t\treturn Result[T, E]{\n62\t\t\tok:    true,\n63\t\t\tvalue: value,\n64\t\t}\n65\t}\n66\t\n67\t// Err creates a failed Result containing the provided error.\n68\t//\n69\t// Example:\n70\t//\tresult := Err[Config](ParseError{FilePath: \"config.yaml\", Line: 5, Message: \"syntax error\"})\n71\t//\tresult := Err[Data](ValidationError{Message: \"required field missing\"})\n72\tfunc Err[T any, E any](err E) Result[T, E] {\n73\t\treturn Result[T, E]{\n74\t\t\tok:  false,\n75\t\t\terr: err,\n76\t\t}\n77\t}\n78\t\n79\t// IsOk returns true if the Result is successful (contains a value).\n80\tfunc (r Result[T, E]) IsOk() bool {\n81\t\treturn r.ok\n82\t}\n83\t\n84\t// IsErr returns true if the Result is failed (contains an error).\n85\tfunc (r Result[T, E]) IsErr() bool {\n86\t\treturn !r.ok\n87\t}\n88\t\n89\t// Unwrap returns the contained success value.\n90\t//\n91\t// Panics if the Result is an error. Use IsOk() to check before calling.\n92\t//\n93\t// This is typically used when you've already verified the Result is Ok:\n94\t//\n95\t//\tif result.IsOk() {\n96\t//\t    value := result.Unwrap()\n97\t//\t    // Use value\n98\t//\t}\n99\tfunc (r Result[T, E]) Unwrap() T {\n100\t\tif r.ok {\n101\t\t\treturn r.value\n102\t\t}\n103\t\t// Panic with a helpful message\n104\t\tpanic(\"unwrap called on Err result: \" + stringifyError(r.err))\n105\t}\n106\t\n107\t// UnwrapErr returns the contained error.\n108\t//\n109\t// Panics if the Result is Ok. Use IsErr() to check before calling.\n110\t//\n111\t// This is typically used when you've already verified the Result is an error:\n112\t//\n113\t//\tif result.IsErr() {\n114\t//\t    err := result.UnwrapErr()\n115\t//\t    // Handle error\n116\t//\t}\n117\tfunc (r Result[T, E]) UnwrapErr() E {\n118\t\tif !r.ok {\n119\t\t\treturn r.err\n120\t\t}\n121\t\tpanic(\"unwrap_err called on Ok result\")\n122\t}\n123\t\n124\t// UnwrapOrDefault returns the contained success value, or the zero value for T if Err.\n125\t//\n126\t// This is useful when you want to provide a default value instead of panicking.\n127\tfunc (r Result[T, E]) UnwrapOrDefault() T {\n128\t\tif r.ok {\n129\t\t\treturn r.value\n130\t\t}\n131\t\tvar zero T\n132\t\treturn zero\n133\t}\n134\t\n135\t// UnwrapOr returns the contained success value, or the provided default value if Err.\n136\t//\n137\t// Example:\n138\t//\tresult := ParseOptionalField(data)\n139\t//\tvalue := result.UnwrapOr(\"default\")\n140\tfunc (r Result[T, E]) UnwrapOr(defaultValue T) T {\n141\t\tif r.ok {\n142\t\t\treturn r.value\n143\t\t}\n144\t\treturn defaultValue\n145\t}\n146\t\n147\t// UnwrapOrElse returns the contained success value, or computes a value from the provided function.\n148\t//\n149\t// Example:\n150\t//\tresult := ParseConfig(data)\n151\t//\tconfig := result.UnwrapOrElse(func() Config {\n152\t//\t    log.Println(\"Using default config\")\n153\t//\t    return DefaultConfig()\n154\t//\t})\n155\tfunc (r Result[T, E]) UnwrapOrElse(f func() T) T {\n156\t\tif r.ok {\n157\t\t\treturn r.value\n158\t\t}\n159\t\treturn f()\n160\t}\n161\t\n162\t// Map applies a function to the contained success value, transforming it.\n163\t//\n164\t// If the Result is Ok, the function is applied to the value and a new Ok is returned.\n165\t// If the Result is Err, the error is propagated unchanged.\n166\t//\n167\t// Example:\n168\t//\tresult := ParseInt(\"42\")\n169\t//\tdoubled := result.Map(func(n int) int { return n * 2 })\n170\tfunc (r Result[T, E]) Map(f func(T) T) Result[T, E] {\n171\t\tif r.ok {\n172\t\t\treturn Ok[T, E](f(r.value))\n173\t\t}\n174\t\treturn Err[T, E](r.err)\n175\t}\n176\t\n177\t// MapErr applies a function to the contained error, transforming it.\n178\t//\n179\t// If the Result is Err, the function is applied to the error and a new Err is returned.\n180\t// If the Result is Ok, the value is propagated unchanged.\n181\t//\n182\t// Example:\n183\t//\tresult := ParseConfig(data)\n184\t//\tannotated := result.MapErr(func(e ParseError) ParseError {\n185\t//\t    e.ContextStr = \"while parsing main config\"\n186\t//\t    return e\n187\t//\t})\n188\tfunc (r Result[T, E]) MapErr(f func(E) E) Result[T, E] {\n189\t\tif r.ok {\n190\t\t\treturn Ok[T, E](r.value)\n191\t\t}\n192\t\treturn Err[T, E](f(r.err))\n193\t}\n194\t\n195\t// AndThen chains operations that can fail, returning the result of the function if Ok.\n196\t//\n197\t// If the Result is Ok, the function is applied to the value and its result is returned.\n198\t// If the Result is Err, the error is propagated unchanged.\n199\t//\n200\t// This is useful for chaining multiple operations that can fail:\n201\t//\n202\t//\tresult := ParseFile(path).AndThen(func(data []byte) Result[Config, ParseError] {\n203\t//\t    return ParseConfig(data)\n204\t//\t})\n205\tfunc (r Result[T, E]) AndThen(f func(T) Result[T, E]) Result[T, E] {\n206\t\tif r.ok {\n207\t\t\treturn f(r.value)\n208\t\t}\n209\t\treturn Err[T, E](r.err)\n210\t}\n211\t\n212\t// OrElse returns the provided Result if this Result is Err, otherwise returns itself.\n213\t//\n214\t// This is useful for providing fallback results:\n215\t//\n216\t//\tresult := TryPrimaryConfig().OrElse(func() Result[Config, ParseError] {\n217\t//\t    return TryFallbackConfig()\n218\t//\t})\n219\tfunc (r Result[T, E]) OrElse(f func(E) Result[T, E]) Result[T, E] {\n220\t\tif r.ok {\n221\t\t\treturn r\n222\t\t}\n223\t\treturn f(r.err)\n224\t}\n225\t\n226\t// Match executes the appropriate function based on the Result state.\n227\t//\n228\t// If Ok, onOk is called with the value.\n229\t// If Err, onErr is called with the error.\n230\t//\n231\t// Example:\n232\t//\tresult := ParseConfig(path)\n233\t//\tresult.Match(\n234\t//\t    func(config Config) {\n235\t//\t        log.Println(\"Config parsed successfully\")\n236\t//\t    },\n237\t//\t    func(err ParseError) {\n238\t//\t        log.Printf(\"Parse failed: %v\", err)\n239\t//\t    },\n240\t//\t)\n241\tfunc (r Result[T, E]) Match(onOk func(T), onErr func(E)) {\n242\t\tif r.ok {\n243\t\t\tonOk(r.value)\n244\t\t} else {\n245\t\t\tonErr(r.err)\n246\t\t}\n247\t}\n248\t\n249\t// Match returns a value based on the Result state.\n250\t//\n251\t// If Ok, onOk is called with the value and its result is returned.\n252\t// If Err, onErr is called with the error and its result is returned.\n253\t//\n254\t// Example:\n255\t//\tresult := ParseConfig(path)\n256\t//\tmessage := result.MatchReturn(\n257\t//\t    func(config Config) string { return \"Success\" },\n258\t//\t    func(err ParseError) string { return \"Failed: \" + err.Error() },\n259\t//\t)\n260\tfunc (r Result[T, E]) MatchReturn(onOk func(T) any, onErr func(E) any) any {\n261\t\tif r.ok {\n262\t\t\treturn onOk(r.value)\n263\t\t}\n264\t\treturn onErr(r.err)\n265\t}\n266\t\n267\t// ToOption converts the Result to an Option[T] (if you had an Option type).\n268\t//\n269\t// Ok(value) becomes Some(value)\n270\t// Err becomes None\n271\t//\n272\t// This is useful when you want to ignore errors and just work with optional values.\n273\tfunc (r Result[T, E]) ToOption() Option[T] {\n274\t\tif r.ok {\n275\t\t\treturn Some[T](r.value)\n276\t\t}\n277\t\treturn None[T]()\n278\t}\n279\t\n280\t// Error returns the error as an error interface, or nil if Ok.\n281\t//\n282\t// This allows Result types to satisfy the error interface indirectly.\n283\tfunc (r Result[T, E]) Error() error {\n284\t\tif r.ok {\n285\t\t\treturn nil\n286\t\t}\n287\t\t// Try to convert E to error interface\n288\t\tif err, ok := any(r.err).(error); ok {\n289\t\t\treturn err\n290\t\t}\n291\t\treturn nil\n292\t}\n293\t\n294\t// String returns a string representation of the Result.\n295\tfunc (r Result[T, E]) String() string {\n296\t\tif r.ok {\n297\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"Ok(%v)\", r.value)\n298\t\t}\n299\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"Err(%v)\", r.err)\n300\t}\n301\t\n302\t// ============================================================================\n303\t// Helper functions for common patterns\n304\t// ============================================================================\n305\t\n306\t// CollectResults collects multiple Results into a single Result.\n307\t//\n308\t// If all Results are Ok, returns Ok with a slice of all values.\n309\t// If any Result is Err, returns Err with the first error encountered.\n310\t//\n311\t// Example:\n312\t//\tresults := []Result[int, ParseError]{Ok(1), Ok(2), Ok(3)}\n313\t//\tcombined := CollectResults(results)\n314\t//\t// combined is Ok([]int{1, 2, 3})\n315\tfunc CollectResults[T any, E any](results []Result[T, E]) Result[[]T, E] {\n316\t\tvalues := make([]T, 0, len(results))\n317\t\tfor _, result := range results {\n318\t\t\tif result.IsOk() {\n319\t\t\t\tvalues = append(values, result.Unwrap())\n320\t\t\t} else {\n321\t\t\t\treturn Err[[]T, E](result.UnwrapErr())\n322\t\t\t}\n323\t\t}\n324\t\treturn Ok[[]T, E](values)\n325\t}\n326\t\n327\t// PartitionResults separates Results into Ok and Err groups.\n328\t//\n329\t// Returns two slices: all successful values and all errors.\n330\t//\n331\t// Example:\n332\t//\tresults := []Result[int, ParseError]{Ok(1), Err(...), Ok(2)}\n333\t//\toks, errs := PartitionResults(results)\n334\t//\t// 0)\n339\t\tfor _, result := range results {\n340\t\t\tif result.IsOk() {\n341\t\t\t\toks = append(oks, result.Unwrap())\n342\t\t\t} else {\n343\t\t\t\terrs = append(errs, result.UnwrapErr())\n344\t\t\t}\n345\t\t}\n346\t\treturn oks, errs\n347\t}\n348\t\n349\t// Transpose converts a Result of Option to an Option of Result.\n350\t//\n351\t// Ok(Some(x)) -> Some(Ok(x))\n352\t// Ok(None) -> None\n353\t// Err(e) -> Some(Err(e))\n354\t//\n355\t// This is useful when working with nested optional/error results.\n356\tfunc Transpose[T any, E any](r Result[Option[T], E]) Option[Result[T, E]] {\n357\t\tif r.IsErr() {\n358\t\t\treturn Some(Result[T, E]{ok: false, err: r.UnwrapErr()})\n359\t\t}\n360\t\topt := r.Unwrap()\n361\t\tif opt.IsSome() {\n362\t\t\treturn Some(Ok[T, E](opt.Unwrap()))\n363\t\t}\n364\t\treturn None[Result[T, E]]()\n365\t}\n366\t\n367\t// ============================================================================\n368\t// Integration with existing error types\n369\t// ============================================================================\n370\t\n371\t// AsParseError converts any error to a ParseError.\n372\t//\n373\t// If the error is already a ParseError, it's returned as-is.\n374\t// If it's a YAMLError, it's converted to a ParseError.\n375\t// Otherwise, a generic ParseError is created.\n376\tfunc AsParseError(err error) *ParseError {\n377\t\tif err == nil {\n378\t\t\treturn nil\n379\t\t}\n380\t\n381\t\t// Already a ParseError\n382\t\tif pe, ok := err.(*ParseError); ok {\n383\t\t\treturn pe\n384\t\t}\n385\t\n386\t\t// Try to extract info from YAMLError types\n387\t\tif ye, ok := err.(YAMLError); ok {\n388\t\t\t// Extract line/column info if available\n389\t\t\tline, column := 0, 0\n390\t\t\tfilePath := \"\"\n391\t\t\tmessage := err.Error()\n392\t\n393\t\t\t// Try to get location info from specific error types\n394\t\t\tswitch e := err.(type) {\n395\t\t\tcase *SyntaxError:\n396\t\t\t\tline, column = e.Line, e.Column\n397\t\t\t\tfilePath = e.FilePath\n398\t\t\t\tmessage = e.Message\n399\t\t\tcase *StructureError:\n400\t\t\t\tline = e.Line\n401\t\t\t\tfilePath = e.FilePath\n402\t\t\t\tmessage = e.Message\n403\t\t\tcase *TypeMismatchError:\n404\t\t\t\tline = e.Line\n405\t\t\t\tfilePath = e.FilePath\n406\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"type mismatch at %s: expected %s, got %s\",\n407\t\t\t\t\te.FieldPath, e.ExpectedType, e.ActualType)\n408\t\t\tcase *ValidationError:\n409\t\t\t\tline, column = e.Line, e.Column\n410\t\t\t\tfilePath = e.FilePath\n411\t\t\t\tmessage = e.Message\n412\t\t\t}\n413\t\n414\t\t\treturn &ParseError{\n415\t\t\t\tFilePath:  filePath,\n416\t\t\t\tLine:      line,\n417\t\t\t\tColumn:    column,\n418\t\t\t\tMessage:   message,\n419\t\t\t\tErr:       err,\n420\t\t\t\tErrorType: ye.YAMLErrorType(),\n421\t\t\t\tErrorCode: ye.Code(),\n422\t\t\t}\n423\t\t}\n424\t\n425\t\t// Generic error - create basic ParseError\n426\t\treturn &ParseError{\n427\t\t\tMessage: err.Error(),\n428\t\t\tErr:     err,\n429\t\t}\n430\t}\n431\t\n432\t// FromError converts a standard Go error to a Result[T, ParseError].\n433\t//\n434\t// If err is nil, returns Ok with the provided value.\n435\t// If err is non-nil, converts it to a ParseError and returns Err.\n436\tfunc FromError[T any](value T, err error) Result[T, *ParseError] {\n437\t\tif err == nil {\n438\t\t\treturn Ok[T, *ParseError](value)\n439\t\t}\n440\t\treturn Err[T, *ParseError](AsParseError(err))\n441\t}\n442\t\n443\t// WithLineNumber adds line number information to a ParseError.\n444\t//\n445\t// If the result is Ok, returns it unchanged.\n446\t// If the result is Err, adds the line number to the error.\n447\tfunc WithLineNumber[T any](r Result[T, *ParseError], *ParseError](err)\n456\t}\n457\t\n458\t// WithContext adds context information to a ParseError.\n459\t//\n460\t// If the result is Ok, returns it unchanged.\n461\t// If the result is Err, adds the context string to the error.\n462\tfunc WithContext[T any](r Result[T, *ParseError], context string) Result[T, *ParseError] {\n463\t\tif r.IsOk() {\n464\t\t\treturn r\n465\t\t}\n466\t\terr := r.UnwrapErr()\n467\t\tif err.ContextStr == \"\" {\n468\t\t\terr.ContextStr = context\n469\t\t} else {\n470\t\t\terr.ContextStr = context + \": \" + err.ContextStr\n471\t\t}\n472\t\treturn Err[T, *ParseError](err)\n473\t}\n474\t\n475\t// ============================================================================\n476\t// Option type (for ToOption conversion)\n477\t// ============================================================================\n478\t\n479\t// Option represents an optional value that can be Some(T) or None.\n480\t//\n481\t// This is a simple option type used for interoperability with Result.\n482\ttype Option[T any] struct {\n483\t\tsome bool\n484\t\tvalue T\n485\t}\n486\t\n487\t// Some creates an Option containing a value.\n488\tfunc Some[T any](value T) Option[T] {\n489\t\treturn Option[T]{some: true, value: value}\n490\t}\n491\t\n492\t// None creates an empty Option.\n493\tfunc None[T any]() Option[T] {\n494\t\treturn Option[T]{some: false}\n495\t}\n496\t\n497\t// IsSome returns true if the Option contains a value.\n498\tfunc (o Option[T]) IsSome() bool {\n499\t\treturn o.some\n500\t}\n501\t\n502\t// IsNone returns true if the Option is empty.\n503\tfunc (o Option[T]) IsNone() bool {\n504\t\treturn !o.some\n505\t}\n506\t\n507\t// Unwrap returns the contained value, panics if None.\n508\tfunc (o Option[T]) Unwrap() T {\n509\t\tif o.some {\n510\t\t\treturn o.value\n511\t\t}\n512\t\tpanic(\"unwrap called on None\")\n513\t}\n514\t\n515\t// UnwrapOr returns the contained value or the provided default.\n516\tfunc (o Option[T]) UnwrapOr(defaultValue T) T {\n517\t\tif o.some {\n518\t\t\treturn o.value\n519\t\t}\n520\t\treturn defaultValue\n521\t}\n522\t\n523\t// ============================================================================\n524\t// Helper functions\n525\t// ============================================================================\n526\t\n527\t// stringifyError converts an error to a string representation.\n528\tfunc stringifyError(err any) string {\n529\t\tif err == nil {\n530\t\t\treturn \"<nil>\"\n531\t\t}\n532\t\tif e, ok := err.(error); ok {\n533\t\t\treturn e.Error()\n534\t\t}\n535\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", Package yamlutil provides Rust-style Result type for error handling.\n//\n// The Result[T, E] type represents either success (Ok) containing a value of type T,\n// or failure (Err) containing an error of type E.\n//\n// This pattern enables explicit error handling without exceptions, making error\n// paths visible in the type system and forcing proper error handling.\npackage yamlutil\n\nimport \"fmt\"\n\n// Result represents the outcome of an operation that can either succeed with a value\n// or fail with an error.\n//\n// Result is modeled after Rust's Result<T, E> type and provides a type-safe way to\n// handle operations that can fail without using exceptions.\n//\n// Type parameters:\n//   - T: The success value type\n//   - E: The error type (typically ParseError, ValidationError, or any YAMLError)\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\t// A function that returns Result[Config, ParseError]\n//\tfunc ParseConfig(path string) Result[Config, ParseError] {\n//\t    data, err := os.ReadFile(path)\n//\t    if err != nil {\n//\t        return Err[Config](ParseError{...})\n//\t    }\n//\t    config := parseConfig(data)\n//\t    return Ok[Config](config)\n//\t}\n//\n//\t// Using the result\n//\tresult := ParseConfig(\"config.yaml\")\n//\tif result.IsOk() {\n//\t    config := result.Unwrap()\n//\t    // Use config\n//\t} else {\n//\t    err := result.UnwrapErr()\n//\t    // Handle error\n//\t}\ntype Result[T any, E any] struct {\n\t// ok is true when the result is successful\n\tok bool\n\n\t// value contains the success value when ok is true\n\tvalue T\n\n\t// err contains the error when ok is false\n\terr E\n}\n\n// Ok creates a successful Result containing the provided value.\n//\n// Example:\n//\tresult := Ok(42)\n//\tresult := Ok(\"success\")\n//\tresult := Ok(myStruct{})\nfunc Ok[T any, E any](value T) Result[T, E] {\n\treturn Result[T, E]{\n\t\tok:    true,\n\t\tvalue: value,\n\t}\n}\n\n// Err creates a failed Result containing the provided error.\n//\n// Example:\n//\tresult := Err[Config](ParseError{FilePath: \"config.yaml\", Line: 5, Message: \"syntax error\"})\n//\tresult := Err[Data](ValidationError{Message: \"required field missing\"})\nfunc Err[T any, E any](err E) Result[T, E] {\n\treturn Result[T, E]{\n\t\tok:  false,\n\t\terr: err,\n\t}\n}\n\n// IsOk returns true if the Result is successful (contains a value).\nfunc (r Result[T, E]) IsOk() bool {\n\treturn r.ok\n}\n\n// IsErr returns true if the Result is failed (contains an error).\nfunc (r Result[T, E]) IsErr() bool {\n\treturn !r.ok\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the contained success value.\n//\n// Panics if the Result is an error. Use IsOk() to check before calling.\n//\n// This is typically used when you've already verified the Result is Ok:\n//\n//\tif result.IsOk() {\n//\t    value := result.Unwrap()\n//\t    // Use value\n//\t}\nfunc (r Result[T, E]) Unwrap() T {\n\tif r.ok {\n\t\treturn r.value\n\t}\n\t// Panic with a helpful message\n\tpanic(\"unwrap called on Err result: \" + stringifyError(r.err))\n}\n\n// UnwrapErr returns the contained error.\n//\n// Panics if the Result is Ok. Use IsErr() to check before calling.\n//\n// This is typically used when you've already verified the Result is an error:\n//\n//\tif result.IsErr() {\n//\t    err := result.UnwrapErr()\n//\t    // Handle error\n//\t}\nfunc (r Result[T, E]) UnwrapErr() E {\n\tif !r.ok {\n\t\treturn r.err\n\t}\n\tpanic(\"unwrap_err called on Ok result\")\n}\n\n// UnwrapOrDefault returns the contained success value, or the zero value for T if Err.\n//\n// This is useful when you want to provide a default value instead of panicking.\nfunc (r Result[T, E]) UnwrapOrDefault() T {\n\tif r.ok {\n\t\treturn r.value\n\t}\n\tvar zero T\n\treturn zero\n}\n\n// UnwrapOr returns the contained success value, or the provided default value if Err.\n//\n// Example:\n//\tresult := ParseOptionalField(data)\n//\tvalue := result.UnwrapOr(\"default\")\nfunc (r Result[T, E]) UnwrapOr(defaultValue T) T {\n\tif r.ok {\n\t\treturn r.value\n\t}\n\treturn defaultValue\n}\n\n// UnwrapOrElse returns the contained success value, or computes a value from the provided function.\n//\n// Example:\n//\tresult := ParseConfig(data)\n//\tconfig := result.UnwrapOrElse(func() Config {\n//\t    log.Println(\"Using default config\")\n//\t    return DefaultConfig()\n//\t})\nfunc (r Result[T, E]) UnwrapOrElse(f func() T) T {\n\tif r.ok {\n\t\treturn r.value\n\t}\n\treturn f()\n}\n\n// Map applies a function to the contained success value, transforming it.\n//\n// If the Result is Ok, the function is applied to the value and a new Ok is returned.\n// If the Result is Err, the error is propagated unchanged.\n//\n// Example:\n//\tresult := ParseInt(\"42\")\n//\tdoubled := result.Map(func(n int) int { return n * 2 })\nfunc (r Result[T, E]) Map(f func(T) T) Result[T, E] {\n\tif r.ok {\n\t\treturn Ok[T, E](f(r.value))\n\t}\n\treturn Err[T, E](r.err)\n}\n\n// MapErr applies a function to the contained error, transforming it.\n//\n// If the Result is Err, the function is applied to the error and a new Err is returned.\n// If the Result is Ok, the value is propagated unchanged.\n//\n// Example:\n//\tresult := ParseConfig(data)\n//\tannotated := result.MapErr(func(e ParseError) ParseError {\n//\t    e.ContextStr = \"while parsing main config\"\n//\t    return e\n//\t})\nfunc (r Result[T, E]) MapErr(f func(E) E) Result[T, E] {\n\tif r.ok {\n\t\treturn Ok[T, E](r.value)\n\t}\n\treturn Err[T, E](f(r.err))\n}\n\n// AndThen chains operations that can fail, returning the result of the function if Ok.\n//\n// If the Result is Ok, the function is applied to the value and its result is returned.\n// If the Result is Err, the error is propagated unchanged.\n//\n// This is useful for chaining multiple operations that can fail:\n//\n//\tresult := ParseFile(path).AndThen(func(data []byte) Result[Config, ParseError] {\n//\t    return ParseConfig(data)\n//\t})\nfunc (r Result[T, E]) AndThen(f func(T) Result[T, E]) Result[T, E] {\n\tif r.ok {\n\t\treturn f(r.value)\n\t}\n\treturn Err[T, E](r.err)\n}\n\n// OrElse returns the provided Result if this Result is Err, otherwise returns itself.\n//\n// This is useful for providing fallback results:\n//\n//\tresult := TryPrimaryConfig().OrElse(func() Result[Config, ParseError] {\n//\t    return TryFallbackConfig()\n//\t})\nfunc (r Result[T, E]) OrElse(f func(E) Result[T, E]) Result[T, E] {\n\tif r.ok {\n\t\treturn r\n\t}\n\treturn f(r.err)\n}\n\n// Match executes the appropriate function based on the Result state.\n//\n// If Ok, onOk is called with the value.\n// If Err, onErr is called with the error.\n//\n// Example:\n//\tresult := ParseConfig(path)\n//\tresult.Match(\n//\t    func(config Config) {\n//\t        log.Println(\"Config parsed successfully\")\n//\t    },\n//\t    func(err ParseError) {\n//\t        log.Printf(\"Parse failed: %v\", err)\n//\t    },\n//\t)\nfunc (r Result[T, E]) Match(onOk func(T), onErr func(E)) {\n\tif r.ok {\n\t\tonOk(r.value)\n\t} else {\n\t\tonErr(r.err)\n\t}\n}\n\n// Match returns a value based on the Result state.\n//\n// If Ok, onOk is called with the value and its result is returned.\n// If Err, onErr is called with the error and its result is returned.\n//\n// Example:\n//\tresult := ParseConfig(path)\n//\tmessage := result.MatchReturn(\n//\t    func(config Config) string { return \"Success\" },\n//\t    func(err ParseError) string { return \"Failed: \" + err.Error() },\n//\t)\nfunc (r Result[T, E]) MatchReturn(onOk func(T) any, onErr func(E) any) any {\n\tif r.ok {\n\t\treturn onOk(r.value)\n\t}\n\treturn onErr(r.err)\n}\n\n// ToOption converts the Result to an Option[T] (if you had an Option type).\n//\n// Ok(value) becomes Some(value)\n// Err becomes None\n//\n// This is useful when you want to ignore errors and just work with optional values.\nfunc (r Result[T, E]) ToOption() Option[T] {\n\tif r.ok {\n\t\treturn Some[T](r.value)\n\t}\n\treturn None[T]()\n}\n\n// Error returns the error as an error interface, or nil if Ok.\n//\n// This allows Result types to satisfy the error interface indirectly.\nfunc (r Result[T, E]) Error() error {\n\tif r.ok {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\t// Try to convert E to error interface\n\tif err, ok := any(r.err).(error); ok {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// String returns a string representation of the Result.\nfunc (r Result[T, E]) String() string {\n\tif r.ok {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"Ok(%v)\", r.value)\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"Err(%v)\", r.err)\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Helper functions for common patterns\n// ============================================================================\n\n// CollectResults collects multiple Results into a single Result.\n//\n// If all Results are Ok, returns Ok with a slice of all values.\n// If any Result is Err, returns Err with the first error encountered.\n//\n// Example:\n//\tresults := []Result[int, ParseError]{Ok(1), Ok(2), Ok(3)}\n//\tcombined := CollectResults(results)\n//\t// combined is Ok([]int{1, 2, 3})\nfunc CollectResults[T any, E any](results []Result[T, E]) Result[[]T, E] {\n\tvalues := make([]T, 0, len(results))\n\tfor _, result := range results {\n\t\tif result.IsOk() {\n\t\t\tvalues = append(values, result.Unwrap())\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\treturn Err[[]T, E](result.UnwrapErr())\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn Ok[[]T, E](values)\n}\n\n// PartitionResults separates Results into Ok and Err groups.\n//\n// Returns two slices: all successful values and all errors.\n//\n// Example:\n//\tresults := []Result[int, ParseError]{Ok(1), Err(...), Ok(2)}\n//\toks, errs := PartitionResults(results)\n//\t// oks = []int{1, 2}\n//\t// errs = []ParseError{...}\nfunc PartitionResults[T any, E any](results []Result[T, E]) ([]T, []E) {\n\toks := make([]T, 0)\n\terrs := make([]E, 0)\n\tfor _, result := range results {\n\t\tif result.IsOk() {\n\t\t\toks = append(oks, result.Unwrap())\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\terrs = append(errs, result.UnwrapErr())\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn oks, errs\n}\n\n// Transpose converts a Result of Option to an Option of Result.\n//\n// Ok(Some(x)) -> Some(Ok(x))\n// Ok(None) -> None\n// Err(e) -> Some(Err(e))\n//\n// This is useful when working with nested optional/error results.\nfunc Transpose[T any, E any](r Result[Option[T], E]) Option[Result[T, E]] {\n\tif r.IsErr() {\n\t\treturn Some(Result[T, E]{ok: false, err: r.UnwrapErr()})\n\t}\n\topt := r.Unwrap()\n\tif opt.IsSome() {\n\t\treturn Some(Ok[T, E](opt.Unwrap()))\n\t}\n\treturn None[Result[T, E]]()\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Integration with existing error types\n// ============================================================================\n\n// AsParseError converts any error to a ParseError.\n//\n// If the error is already a ParseError, it's returned as-is.\n// If it's a YAMLError, it's converted to a ParseError.\n// Otherwise, a generic ParseError is created.\nfunc AsParseError(err error) *ParseError {\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Already a ParseError\n\tif pe, ok := err.(*ParseError); ok {\n\t\treturn pe\n\t}\n\n\t// Try to extract info from YAMLError types\n\tif ye, ok := err.(YAMLError); ok {\n\t\t// Extract line/column info if available\n\t\tline, column := 0, 0\n\t\tfilePath := \"\"\n\t\tmessage := err.Error()\n\n\t\t// Try to get location info from specific error types\n\t\tswitch e := err.(type) {\n\t\tcase *SyntaxError:\n\t\t\tline, column = e.Line, e.Column\n\t\t\tfilePath = e.FilePath\n\t\t\tmessage = e.Message\n\t\tcase *StructureError:\n\t\t\tline = e.Line\n\t\t\tfilePath = e.FilePath\n\t\t\tmessage = e.Message\n\t\tcase *TypeMismatchError:\n\t\t\tline = e.Line\n\t\t\tfilePath = e.FilePath\n\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"type mismatch at %s: expected %s, got %s\",\n\t\t\t\te.FieldPath, e.ExpectedType, e.ActualType)\n\t\tcase *ValidationError:\n\t\t\tline, column = e.Line, e.Column\n\t\t\tfilePath = e.FilePath\n\t\t\tmessage = e.Message\n\t\t}\n\n\t\treturn &ParseError{\n\t\t\tFilePath:  filePath,\n\t\t\tLine:      line,\n\t\t\tColumn:    column,\n\t\t\tMessage:   message,\n\t\t\tErr:       err,\n\t\t\tErrorType: ye.YAMLErrorType(),\n\t\t\tErrorCode: ye.Code(),\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Generic error - create basic ParseError\n\treturn &ParseError{\n\t\tMessage: err.Error(),\n\t\tErr:     err,\n\t}\n}\n\n// FromError converts a standard Go error to a Result[T, ParseError].\n//\n// If err is nil, returns Ok with the provided value.\n// If err is non-nil, converts it to a ParseError and returns Err.\nfunc FromError[T any](value T, err error) Result[T, *ParseError] {\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\treturn Ok[T, *ParseError](value)\n\t}\n\treturn Err[T, *ParseError](AsParseError(err))\n}\n\n// WithLineNumber *ParseError], line int) Result[T, *ParseError] {\n\tif r.IsOk() {\n\t\treturn r\n\t}\n\terr := r.UnwrapErr()\n\tif err.Line == 0 {\n\t\terr.Line = line\n\t}\n\treturn Err[T, *ParseError](err)\n}\n\n// Result[T, *ParseError], context string) Result[T, *ParseError] {\n\tif r.IsOk() {\n\t\treturn r\n\t}\n\terr := r.UnwrapErr()\n\tif err.ContextStr == \"\" {\n\t\terr.ContextStr = context\n\t} else {\n\t\terr.ContextStr = context + \": \" + err.ContextStr\n\t}\n\treturn Err[T, *ParseError](err)\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Option type (for ToOption conversion)\n// ============================================================================\n\n// Option represents an optional value that can be Some(T) or None.\n//\n// This is a simple option type used for interoperability with Result.\ntype Option[T any] struct {\n\tsome bool\n\tvalue T\n}\n\n// Some creates an Option containing a value.\nfunc Some[T any](value T) Option[T] {\n\treturn Option[T]{some: true, value: value}\n}\n\n// None creates an empty Option.\nfunc None[T any]() Option[T] {\n\treturn Option[T]{some: false}\n}\n\n// IsSome returns true if the Option contains a value.\nfunc (o Option[T]) IsSome() bool {\n\treturn o.some\n}\n\n// IsNone returns true if the Option is empty.\nfunc (o Option[T]) IsNone() bool {\n\treturn !o.some\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the contained value, panics if None.\nfunc (o Option[T]) Unwrap() T {\n\tif o.some {\n\t\treturn o.value\n\t}\n\tpanic(\"unwrap called on None\")\n}\n\n// UnwrapOr returns the contained value or the provided default.\nfunc (o Option[T]) UnwrapOr(defaultValue T) T {\n\tif o.some {\n\t\treturn o.value\n\t}\n\treturn defaultValue\n}\n\n// implements the Result type that integrates with EnhancedParseError\n// for compile-time guaranteed error handling.\npackage yamlutil\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n// ParseResultWithError[T] represents a parsing operation result that can be either\n// a success with data or a failure with EnhancedParseError.\n//\n// This type enforces error handling at compile time - callers must check the error\n// before accessing data, eliminating nil pointer dereference bugs.\n//\n// Type parameter T is the type of successfully parsed data.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tresult := ParseYAMLToFile[Config](filePath)\n//\tif result.IsError() {\n//\t    log.Printf(\"Parse failed: %v\", result.Error)\n//\t    return\n//\t}\n//\tconfig := result.Unwrap()\ntype ParseResultWithError[T any] struct {\n\t// success indicates whether the operation succeeded\n\tsuccess bool\n\n\t// data contains the successful result (only valid when success is true)\n\tdata T\n\n\t// err contains the parse error (only valid when success is false)\n\terr *EnhancedParseError\n}\n\n// OkParse creates a successful result with data.\nfunc OkParse[T any](data T) ParseResultWithError[T] {\n\treturn ParseResultWithError[T]{\n\t\tsuccess: true,\n\t\tdata:    data,\n\t}\n}\n\n// ErrParse creates a failed result with an error.\nfunc ErrParse[T any](err *EnhancedParseError) ParseResultWithError[T] {\n\treturn ParseResultWithError[T]{\n\t\tsuccess: false,\n\t\terr:     err,\n\t}\n}\n\n// IsError returns true if this result represents a failure.\nfunc (r ParseResultWithError[T]) IsError() bool {\n\treturn !r.success\n}\n\n// IsOk returns true if this result represents success.\nfunc (r ParseResultWithError[T]) IsOk() bool {\n\treturn r.success\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the successful data.\n// Panics if the result is an error - always check IsOk() first.\nfunc (r ParseResultWithError[T]) Unwrap() T {\n\tif r.IsError() {\n\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"called Unwrap on error result: %v\", r.err))\n\t}\n\treturn r.data\n}\n\n// UnwrapOr returns the successful data, or the provided default value if error.\nfunc (r ParseResultWithError[T]) UnwrapOr(defaultValue T) T {\n\tif r.IsError() {\n\t\treturn defaultValue\n\t}\n\treturn r.data\n}\n\n// UnwrapOrElse returns the successful data, or calls the provided function to\n// compute a default value if error.\nfunc (r ParseResultWithError[T]) UnwrapOrElse(fn func(*EnhancedParseError) T) T {\n\tif r.IsError() {\n\t\treturn fn(r.err)\n\t}\n\treturn r.data\n}\n\n// Error returns the parse error if this result is an error, nil otherwise.\nfunc (r ParseResultWithError[T]) Error() *EnhancedParseError {\n\tif r.IsError() {\n\t\treturn (r ParseResultWithError[T]) Map(fn func(T) T) ParseResultWithError[T] {\n\tif r.IsError() {\n\t\treturn r\n\t}\n\treturn OkParse(fn(r.data))\n}\n\n// MapErr applies a function to the error, successful results\n\tSuccessCount int\n\n\t// ErrorCount is the number of error results\n\tErrorCount int\n}\n\n// CollectParseResults collects a slice of Results into ParseResults with statistics.\nfunc CollectParseResults[T any](results []ParseResultWithError[T]) ParseResults[T] {\n\tsuccessCount := 0\n\terrorCount := 0\n\n\tfor _, r := range results {\n\t\tif r.IsOk() errorCount,\n\t}\n}\n\n// FilterErrors returns only the error results.\nfunc (pr ParseResults[T]) FilterErrors() []ParseResultWithError[T] {\n\tvar errors []ParseResultWithError[T]\n\tfor _, r := token\",\n\t\t5,\n\t\t10,\n\t\t\":\",\n\t\t\"}\",\n\t)\n\n\tif err.Kind != ParseErrorKindSyntax {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected Kind=%s, got %s\", ParseErrorKindSyntax, err.Kind)\n\t}\n\n\tif err.FilePath != \"test.yaml\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected FilePath='test.yaml', got '%s'\", err.FilePath)\n\t}\n\n\tif err.LocationInfo.Line != 5 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected Line=5, got %d\", err.LocationInfo.Line)\n\t}\n\n\tif err.LocationInfo.Column != 10 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected Column=10, got %d\", err.LocationInfo.Column)\n\t}\n\n\tif err.Detail.Expected != \":\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected Expected=':', got '%s'\", err.Detail.Expected)\n\t}\n\n\tif err.Detail.Found != \"}\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected Found='}', got '%s'\", err.Detail.Found)\n\t}\n\n\terrMsg := got: %s\", errMsg)\n\t}\n\n\tif !strings.Contains(errMsg, \"expected: :\") {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected error message to contain 'expected: :', got: %s\", errMsg)\n\t}\n\n\tif !strings.Contains(errMsg, ============================================================================\n// EnhancedParseError Kind Checking Tests\n// ============================================================================\n\nfunc TestEnhancedParseErrorKindCheckers(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname      string\n\t\terr \"\"),\n\t\t\t(*EnhancedParseError).IsStructureError, false},\n\t\t{\"structure error is structure\", NewStructureParseError(\"test.yaml\", \"err\", 1, \"\", \"\"),\n\t\t\t(*EnhancedParseError).IsStructureError, true},\n\t\t{\"structure error is not syntax\", NewStructureParseError(\"test.yaml\", \"err\", 1, \"\",
